name: Hypothalamic NF-kappaB inhibition and GnRH slowed mouse aging phenotypes
summary: Hypothalamic immune-neuroendocrine manipulation slowed systemic aging phenotypes in mice, with GnRH restoration contributing to neurogenesis and aging-phenotype effects.
statement: In mice, preventing aging-related hypothalamic or brain IKK-beta/NF-kappaB activation was reported to retard aging and extend lifespan, while GnRH treatment improved aging-impaired neurogenesis and decelerated aging phenotypes.
time_horizon: Aging-phenotype and lifespan follow-up across multiple mouse interventional models
quantitative_note: The source reports lifespan extension and aging retardation in mouse models; this finding is treated as animal evidence only.
caveats: The interventions were central mouse manipulations and are not directly clinical protocols.
The source does not establish safe chronic GnRH or hypothalamic NF-kappaB intervention in older humans.
Inflammatory and reproductive-axis effects create safety and mechanism boundaries for translation.
